name: MS-QRD-007: Suspicious Account Manipulation — Persistence via Account Changes
description: Detects unauthorized account manipulation activities used to establish persistence or escalate privileges. Monitors for a cluster of account-related events within a short window including: user account creation, account enablement after being disabled, password resets not initiated by the account owner, group membership changes adding users to privileged groups, and account attribute modifications. Attackers manipulate accounts to maintain persistence after initial compromise, create backdoor accounts, or elevate existing low-privilege accounts.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Admin Source IP", username AS "Acting Account", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", CATEGORYNAME(category) AS "Event Category", QIDNAME(qid) AS "Event Type", COUNT(*) AS "Event Count", COUNT(DISTINCT QIDNAME(qid)) AS "Distinct Event Types", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen" FROM events WHERE (CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%account created%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%account enabled%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%account changed%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%password reset%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%group member%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%user account management%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4720%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4722%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4724%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4728%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4732%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4738%') AND username IS NOT NULL AND username != '-' AND username != 'SYSTEM' AND sourceip IS NOT NULL GROUP BY sourceip, username, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid), CATEGORYNAME(category), QIDNAME(qid) HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY "Event Count" DESC LAST 15 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: Multiple account management events from a single source within 15 minutes — especially account creation followed by group membership change — is a strong persistence indicator. Key event sequence to watch: EventID 4720 (account created) → 4722 (account enabled) → 4728/4732 (added to privileged group). This three-step sequence is the standard backdoor account creation pattern. Correlate acting account against known administrators — if non-admin account is performing these actions, immediate investigation is required.
response_actions: Identify and disable any newly created accounts immediately
Audit all account changes made by the acting account in the past 24 hours
Reset password for the acting account if compromise is suspected
Review Domain Admins and Administrators groups for unauthorized members
Check for concurrent logon sessions from acting account across multiple hosts
qradar.false_positive_notes: IT administrators performing legitimate user onboarding (creating accounts and assigning groups). Helpdesk staff performing password resets. Automated provisioning systems (Azure AD Connect, ServiceNow HR integration). Scheduled scripts that manage group memberships. Whitelist known IT admin and helpdesk source IPs in a QRadar Reference Set.
qradar.tuning_notes: COUNT(DISTINCT QIDNAME(qid)) field shows how many different event types occurred — a value of 3+ (create + enable + group add) in sequence is very high confidence. Single event type (COUNT DISTINCT = 1) may be routine admin work. Consider raising threshold to 3+ events AND 2+ distinct event types for higher precision. Add time-of-day filter (outside business hours) to catch after-hours account manipulation which is a major red flag.
